scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked) — masked score is 2 with rules dropped; the 2 diffs are the 5th-mflo dest reg + dependent sra. Full-build SHA1 is the real gate.

reviewer.evidence: No improving candidate to review — candidate.c is HEAD's body (matches only with the 2 regfix rules). No new cheat introduced.

remaining_gap.summary: 2-insn register-allocation plateau on the 5th mflo. The computation `t = (v * (0x1000 - ((s * (0x1000 - f)) >> 12))) >> 12` produces a mult whose result GCC RA places in $8 (t0); TARGET places it in $5 (a1) — the SAME register that held the 0x1000 (`li a1,4096`) constant, reused after its last `subu a1,...` minuend use dies. The dependent `sra` then reads $5 in target vs $8 in our build. 2 regfix subst rules paper over exactly this (mflo dest + sra src).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: The 2 rob_life_ctrl_2 subst rules in regfix.txt:517-521 (mflo \$8->$5 @49, sra $5,\$8,12 -> $5,$5,12 @51). Both must be removed for COMPLETED-C; no pure-C source found that makes GCC reuse the dying a1(=0x1000) for the t-mult result.

next_hypotheses: INSTRUMENTED ALLOCNO DUMP (the named next modality): build tmp/gccdbg/cc1 with BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG / BB2_PRIO_DEBUG (see register-alloc-deep-dive.md) on an isolated standalone of rob_life_ctrl_2 to read the allocno-priority table. Determine why the t-mult pseudo (104 in the prior dump) is NOT given a1 despite no conflict with the 0x1000-constant pseudo at the mflo point. The whole question is liveness: a1(0x1000) must die exactly at t's subu so the mflo can reuse it.

Make the 0x1000 constant's LAST textual use be inside t's subexpression so its live range ends precisely at t's mult (target reuses the dying minuend reg). Care: q also uses 0x1000; the constant must remain CSE'd to one pseudo (a separate `s32 k=0x1000` local risks splitting it).
Try narrowing/retyping the intermediate (s*(0x1000-f))>>12 temp, or hoisting it to a named s32 used once, to change pseudo 104's conflict graph (Lever A/B from register-alloc-pure-c).

If the allocno dump shows the wall is GCC global.c:624 priority tiebreaker (same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec), this is a genuine pure-C wall -> escalate for canonical-asm-vs-park policy. It is NOT obviously hand-coded asm (ordinary HSV->RGB arithmetic), so canonical-asm is unlikely correct.
